summary: DeVault introduces Hooks, a lightweight tool he built to run arbitrary commands when GitHub webhooks fire. The tool was born from needing continuous deployment without a full build server. Configuration is minimal—a simple INI file specifying the repository, branch, command, and allowed IPs. He presents it as a cheap and easy alternative to setting up a proper CI/CD pipeline.
key_points: Hooks fills the gap between no automation and a full build server for simple deployment needs
Configuration is intentionally minimal: repository, branch, command, and IP whitelist
The tool embodies a DIY approach to infrastructure rather than relying on hosted CI services
money_quotes: I found myself in need of a simple tool for deploying a project on every git commit, but I didn't have a build server set up.
This allows you to do continuous deployment on the cheap and easy.
key_insight: Simple problems deserve simple tools—continuous deployment doesn't require a full build server when a lightweight webhook handler will do.
